DIA_KN_TT.name: Testicular Torsion
DIA_KN_TT.description: Testicular torsion occurs when the spermatic cord twists, cutting off the blood supply to the testicle

DIA_KN_EPI.name: Epididymitis
DIA_KN_EPI.description: Epididymitis is an inflammation of the coiled tube the epididymis at the back of the testicle that stores and carries sperm. Males of any age can get epididymitis, although it is more common in younger man.

DIA_KN_EPI_ORCH.name: Epididymitis Orchitis
DIA_KN_EPI_ORCH.description: Epididymitis is an inflammation of the coiled tube the epididymis at the back of the testicle that stores and carries sperm, together with an inflammation and often caused by the testis. Males of any age can get epididymitis orchitis, although it's more common in younger man.

DIA_KN_SM_TSTM_1.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer: Stage I
DIA_KN_SM_TSTM_1.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell. Stage I means that the cancer remains localized to the testis.

DIA_KN_SM_TSTM_2.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer: Stage II
DIA_KN_SM_TSTM_2.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell. Stage II means that the cancer involves the testis and metastasis in the lymph nodes below the diaphragm.

DIA_KN_SM_TSTM_3.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer: Stage III
DIA_KN_SM_TSTM_3.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell. Stage III means that the cancer involves the testis and metastasis in the lymph nodes below as well as above the diaphragm.

DIA_KN_SM_TSTM_4.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer: Stage IV
DIA_KN_SM_TSTM_4.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell. Stage IV means that the cancer has metastasized to other organs.

DIA_KN_NSM_TSTM_1.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer: Stage I
DIA_KN_NSM_TSTM_1.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell. Stage I means that the cancer remains localized to the testis.

DIA_KN_NSM_TSTM_2.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer: Stage II
DIA_KN_NSM_TSTM_2.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell. Stage II means that the cancer involves the testis and metastasis in the lymph nodes below the diaphragm.

DIA_KN_NSM_TSTM_3.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer: Stage III
DIA_KN_NSM_TSTM_3.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell. Stage III means that the cancer involves the testis and metastasis in the lymph nodes below as well as above the diaphragm.

DIA_KN_NSM_TSTM_4.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer: Stage IV
DIA_KN_NSM_TSTM_4.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell. Stage IV means that the cancer has metastasized to other organs.

DIA_KN_BPH_1.name: Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia
DIA_KN_BPH_1.description: Benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), also called prostate enlargement, is a noncancerous increase in size of the prostate gland.

DIA_KN_BPH_2.name: Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia
DIA_KN_BPH_2.description: Benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), also called prostate enlargement, is a noncancerous increase in size of the prostate gland.

DIA_KNUR_ARF.name: Acute Renal Failure -10%
DIA_KNUR_ARF.description: Acute renal failure refers to an abrupt loss of kidney function, effectively seizing the ability to filter the blood and produce urine. A patient with kidney a loss of function of less than 10 percent needs a kidney transplantation. The causes can be many, but are usually due to extensive tissue damage or decreased kidney blood flow.

DIA_KN_STER_WISH.name: Sterilization Wish
DIA_KN_STER_WISH.description: Sterilization wish; Sterilization is any of a number of medical methods of birth control that intentionally leaves a person unable to reproduce. Sterilization procedures are intended to be permanent; reversal is generally difficult or impossible.

DIA_KN_FOURGANG.name: Fournier Gangrene
DIA_KN_FOURGANG.description: Fournier gangrene is a type of necrotizing fasciitis or gangrene affecting the external genitalia or perineum. It commonly occurs in older men, but it can also occur in women and children. It is more likely to occur in diabetics, alcoholics, or those who are immunocompromised.

DIA_KN_CCRCC_1.name: Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma: Stage I
DIA_KN_CCRCC_1.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, or ccRCC, is a type of kidney cancer. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma is named after how the tumor looks under the microscope. The cells in the tumor look clear, like bubbles. Stage I means that the tumor has a diameter of 7 cm or smaller, and limited to the kidney. No lymph node involvement or metastases to distant organs.

DIA_KN_CCRCC_LOC_2.name: Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma: Stage II
DIA_KN_CCRCC_LOC_2.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, or ccRCC, is a type of kidney cancer. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma is named after how the tumor looks under the microscope. The cells in the tumor look clear, like bubbles. Stage II means that the tumor has a diameter bigger than 7 cm, but is still limited to the kidney. No lymph node involvement or metastases to distant organs. In this case the cancer is localized so partial nephrectomy will be enough to remove the tumor.

DIA_KN_CCRCC_SP_2.name: Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma: Stage II
DIA_KN_CCRCC_SP_2.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, or ccRCC, is a type of kidney cancer. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma is named after how the tumor looks under the microscope. The cells in the tumor look clear, like bubbles. Stage II means that the tumor has a diameter bigger than 7 cm, but is still limited to the kidney. No lymph node involvement or metastases to distant organs. In this case the cancer has grown through the Gerota's fascia this is the renal fascia is a layer of connective tissue encapsulating the kidneys and the adrenal glands. Radical nephrectomy will be needed to remove the tumor.

DIA_KN_CCRCC_INFA_3.name: Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma: Stage III
DIA_KN_CCRCC_INFA_3.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, or ccRCC, is a type of kidney cancer. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma is named after how the tumor looks under the microscope. The cells in the tumor look clear, like bubbles. Stage III means it can be a tumor of any size with involvement of a nearby lymph node but no metastases to distant organs. Tumour of this stage may be with or without spread to fatty tissue around the kidney, with or without spread into the large veins leading from the kidney to the heart. In this case the cancer has spread into the inferior Vena Cava.

DIA_KN_CCRCC_3.name: Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma: Stage III
DIA_KN_CCRCC_3.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, or ccRCC, is a type of kidney cancer. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma is named after how the tumor looks under the microscope. The cells in the tumor look clear, like bubbles. Stage III means it can be a tumor of any size with involvement of a nearby lymph node but no metastases to distant organs. Tumour of this stage may be with or without spread to fatty tissue around the kidney, with or without spread into the large veins leading from the kidney to the heart. In this case the cancer has spread into lymph nodes around the kidney.

DIA_KN_CCRCC_4.name: Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma: Stage IV
DIA_KN_CCRCC_4.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, or ccRCC, is a type of kidney cancer. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma is named after how the tumor looks under the microscope. The cells in the tumor look clear, like bubbles. Stage IV means it can be a tumor of any size and has metastasized.

DIA_KN_VVF.name: Vesicovaginal Fistula
DIA_KN_VVF.description: Vesicovaginal fistula, is an abnormal fistulous tract extending between the bladder (vesica) and the vagina that allows the continuous involuntary discharge of urine into the vaginal vault.

DIA_KN_IPRUP.name: Intraperitoneal Bladder Rupture
DIA_KN_IPRUP.description: Intraperitoneal Bladder Rupture: If the tear is on the top of the bladder, the hole will usually open to the part of the abdomen that holds the liver, spleen, and bowel. Urine leaking into the abdomen is a serious problem. This tear can be sewn closed with surgery. A catheter is left in the bladder for up to 2 weeks after surgery to allow the bladder to rest. The tube will either come out through the urethra or out through the skin below the belly button.

DIA_KN_EXRUP_1.name: Extraperitoneal Bladder Rupture
DIA_KN_EXRUP_1.description: Extraperitoneal Bladder Rupture: If the tear is at the bottom or side of the bladder, the urine will leak into the tissues around the bladder instead of the abdominal cavity. Complex injuries of this type should be repaired with surgery. But often it can be treated by simply placing a wide catheter into the bladder to keep it empty. The urine and blood drain into a collection bag. It usually takes at least 10 days for the bladder to heal. The catheter is left in the bladder until an X-ray shows that the leak has sealed. If the catheter doesn’t drain properly, surgery is needed.

DIA_KN_EXRUP_2.name: Extraperitoneal Bladder Rupture
DIA_KN_EXRUP_2.description: Extraperitoneal Bladder Rupture: If the tear is at the bottom or side of the bladder, the urine will leak into the tissues around the bladder instead of the abdominal cavity. Complex injuries of this type should be repaired with surgery. But often it can be treated by simply placing a wide catheter into the bladder to keep it empty. The urine and blood drain into a collection bag. It usually takes at least 10 days for the bladder to heal. The catheter is left in the bladder until an X-ray shows that the leak has sealed. The catheter doesn’t drain properly, surgery is needed!

DIA_KN_PENRUP_1.name: Penetrating Bladder Rupture
DIA_KN_PENRUP_1.description: Penetrating Bladder Rupture: Injury to the bladder from a bullet or other penetrating object is usually fixed with surgery. Most of the time, other organs in the area will be injured and need repair as well. After surgery, a catheter is left in the bladder to drain the urine and blood until the bladder heals.

DIA_KN_WDIA.name: Weekly Dialysis Apointment
DIA_KN_WDIA.description: The patient has chronic kidney failure and has to come in every week for dialysis.

SYM_CRP_GYNECOMASTIA.name: Gynecomastia
SYM_CRP_GYNECOMASTIA.description: Abnormal increase in breast volume in men.

SYM_CRP_HEMATOSPERMIA.name: Hematospermia
SYM_CRP_HEMATOSPERMIA.description: Hematospermia is defined as blood in the semen. While often perceived as having little significance, blood in the ejaculate can cause great concern to the men who experience it. The condition is common, with many episodes going unnoticed; therefore, the prevalence of hematospermia remains unknown.

SYM_CRP_METASTASIZED_CANCER.name: Metastatic Cancer
SYM_CRP_METASTASIZED_CANCER.description: Metastatic cancer is cancer that spreads from its site of origin to another part of the body.

SYM_CRP_PSA_BL_DETECTED.name: Prostate Specific Antigen High Levels
SYM_CRP_PSA_BL_DETECTED.description: Prostate specific antigen (PSA) is a glycoprotein produced solely by the prostate. Its function is to liquefy semen. Small amounts leak into the bloodstream, where it can be measured. Prostate specific antigen is tissue-specific but not cancer-specific. Elevated levels can occur in men with benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), prostatitis, urinary tract infection or prostatic infarction.

SYM_CRP_DIFF_URINE.name: Urinary Retention
SYM_CRP_DIFF_URINE.description: Difficulty urinating and completely emptying the bladder. Urinary retention can have causes that aren't due to underlying disease. Examples include holding in urine intentionally or medication side effects.

SYM_CRP_SUPRACLAVICULAR_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_SUPRACLAVICULAR_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_AXILLARY_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_AXILLARY_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_NECROSIS.name: Necrosis
SYM_CRP_NECROSIS.description: Necrosis is a form of cell injury which results in the premature death of cells in living tissue by autolysis.

SYM_CRP_NIGHT_URGE.name: Frequent Urination at Night
SYM_CRP_NIGHT_URGE.description: A frequent need to get up and go to the bathroom to urinate at night is called nocturia. It differs from enuresis, or bedwetting, in which the person does not arouse from sleep, but the bladder empties anyway. Nocturia is a common cause of sleep loss, especially among older adults.

SYM_KN_SWOLLENTESTIS_WP.name: Painful Swollen Testicle
SYM_KN_SWOLLENTESTIS_WP.description: The testicle on one side is swollen and painful

SYM_KN_SWOLLENTESTIS_WPT.name: Swollen Testicle
SYM_KN_SWOLLENTESTIS_WPT.description: The testicle on one side is swollen, but not painful

SYM_KN_TESTICULARINFARCTION.name: Testicular Infarction
SYM_KN_TESTICULARINFARCTION.description: Testicular damage occurs as a consequence of decreased blood flow, and therefore decreased oxygen and nutrient supply, to the testicle. If the testicle is not viable during surgical exploration, it must be removed to prevent further necrosis, or tissue death.

SYM_KN_HIGHRIDINGTESTIS.name: High-Riding Testicle
SYM_KN_HIGHRIDINGTESTIS.description: The testicle appears to be higher in the scrotum than normal

SYM_KN_ABNORMALLIETESTIS.name: Abnormal Lie of the Testicle
SYM_KN_ABNORMALLIETESTIS.description: The testicle appears to be in an abnormal position

SYM_KN_SUDDENPAINSCROTUM.name: Sudden Pain in Scrotum
SYM_KN_SUDDENPAINSCROTUM.description: The patient experienced a sudden pain in the scrotum

SYM_KN_ABCREMASCTICREFLEX.name: Absence of the Cremasteric Reflex
SYM_KN_ABCREMASCTICREFLEX.description: The cremasteric reflex may be absent with testicular torsion, upper and lower motor neuron disorders, as well as a spine injury of L1-L2. It can also occur if the ilioinguinal nerve has accidentally been cut during a hernia repair.

SYM_KN_CREMASCTICREFLEX.name: The Cremasteric Reflex Is Still Present
SYM_KN_CREMASCTICREFLEX.description: This reflex is elicited by lightly stroking or poking the superior and medial inner part of the thigh regardless of the direction of stroke. The normal response is an immediate contraction of the cremaster muscle that pulls up the testis ipsilaterally on the same side of the body.

SYM_KN_HARD_TESTIS.name: Hardend Testicle
SYM_KN_HARD_TESTIS.description: The testicle is harder than normal

SYM_KN_LUMPTS.name: A Lump or Mass Inside the Testis
SYM_KN_LUMPTS.description: A lump or mass inside the testis has been noticed during physical examination.

SYM_KN_ALFAPRO.name: Tumor Marker Alpha-Fetoprotein
SYM_KN_ALFAPRO.description: Tumor marker alpha-fetoprotein has been noticed in the bloodstream

SYM_KN_BETAHCG.name: Tumor Marker Bèta-HCG
SYM_KN_BETAHCG.description: Tumor marker bèta-HCG has been noticed in the bloodstream

SYM_KN_TSTM.name: Testicular Cancer Is Detected Through Ultrasonography.
SYM_KN_TSTM.description: Testicular cancer is detected through ultrasonography. The differential diagnosis of testicular cancer requires examining the histology of tissue obtained from an inguinal orchiectomy. A biopsy should not be performed, as it raises the risk of spreading cancer cells into the scrotum.

SYM_KN_HVSC.name: A Feeling Often Described as "heaviness" in the Scrotum
SYM_KN_HVSC.description: A feeling often described as "heaviness" in the scrotum

SYM_KN_ENLSM_PRO_1.name: An Enlarged Prostate Gland That Is Symmetric and Smooth.
SYM_KN_ENLSM_PRO_1.description: An enlarged prostate gland on rectal examination that is symmetric and smooth supports a diagnosis of BPH.

SYM_KN_DHT.name: High Levels of Dihydrotestosterone
SYM_KN_DHT.description: High levels of Dihydrotestosterone was detected in the bloodstream

SYM_KN_ENLSM_PRO_2.name: An Enlarged Prostate Gland That Is Symmetric and Smooth.
SYM_KN_ENLSM_PRO_2.description: An enlarged prostate gland on rectal examination that is symmetric and smooth supports a diagnosis of BPH.

SYM_KN_RETRO_EJAC.name: Retrograde Ejaculation
SYM_KN_RETRO_EJAC.description: Retrograde ejaculation occurs when semen which would be ejaculated via the urethra is redirected to the urinary bladder. Normally, the sphincter of the bladder contracts before ejaculation, sealing the bladder which besides inhibiting the release of urine also prevents a reflux of seminal fluids into the male bladder during ejaculation. The semen is forced to exit via the urethra, the path of least resistance. When the bladder sphincter does not function properly, retrograde ejaculation may occur.

SYM_KN_URIN_INCO.name: Urinary Incontinence
SYM_KN_URIN_INCO.description: Urinary incontinence, also known as involuntary urination, is any uncontrolled leakage of urine.

SYM_KN_SWOLLENEPIDIDYMIS.name: Swollen and Tender Epididymis
SYM_KN_SWOLLENEPIDIDYMIS.description: The Epididymis, the tube that connects a testicle to a vas deferens in the male reproductive system, is swollen and is tender uppon touching.

SYM_KN_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_KN_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_KN_IMMUN_REACT.name: The Immunesystem Reacts to the New Organ
SYM_KN_IMMUN_REACT.description: The immunesystem reacts to the new organ

SYM_KN_ACUTE_ORG_REJEC.name: The Body Is Rejecting the New Organ
SYM_KN_ACUTE_ORG_REJEC.description: The body is rejecting the new organ. If not acted uppon, the rejection will be fatal

SYM_KN_NEEDS_UROLOGY.name: The Condition Needs Urology
SYM_KN_NEEDS_UROLOGY.description: The condition needs monitoring of an Urologist

SYM_KN_UNPL_ODOR.name: Unpleasant Odor
SYM_KN_UNPL_ODOR.description: An unpleasant odor can be smelled around the patient

SYM_KN_RCC_7.name: Renal Cell Cancer Smaller Than 7 Centimeters Is Detected on a CT-Scan.
SYM_KN_RCC_7.description: Renal cell cancer is detected on a CT-scan. The tumor appears to be is smaller than 7 centimeters.

SYM_KN_RCC_8.name: Renal Cell Cancer Larger Than 7 Centimeters Is Detected on a CT-Scan.
SYM_KN_RCC_8.description: Renal cell cancer is detected on a CT-scan. The tumor appears to be is larger than 7 centimeters.

SYM_KN_FLANK_PAIN.name: Flank Pain
SYM_KN_FLANK_PAIN.description: Flank pain: pain on the side of the body between the hip and ribs.

SYM_KN_ABDOMINAL_MASS.name: Abdominal Mass
SYM_KN_ABDOMINAL_MASS.description: A mass can be felt in the abdomen

SYM_KN_DEPR_ERYTHR.name: Depression of Erythropoietin
SYM_KN_DEPR_ERYTHR.description: Depression of erythropoietin. Erythropoietin  stimulates red blood cell production (erythropoiesis) in the bone marrow. A depression of erythropoietin gives anemia.

SYM_UR_HYPERCALCEMIA.name: Hypercalcemia
SYM_UR_HYPERCALCEMIA.description: Hypercalcemia is a condition in which the calcium level in your blood is above normal. Hypercalcaemia is most often caused by overactivity in the four tiny glands in the neck (parathyroid glands) or from cancer. Extra calcium in the blood affects many bodily systems.

SYM_KN_RCC_INF_VENACAVA.name: Metastised Tumor in the Inferior Vena Cava. The Inferior Vena Cava (or IVC) Is a Large Vein That Carries the Deoxygenated Blood From the Lower and Middle Body Into the Right Atrium of the Heart. It Is Formed by the Joining of the Right and the Left Common Iliac Veins, Usually at the Level of the Fifth Lumbar Vertebra.
SYM_KN_RCC_INF_VENACAVA.description: Metastised tumor in the Inferior Vena Cava

SYM_KN_BLOODY_URINE.name: Blood Clots in Urine
SYM_KN_BLOODY_URINE.description: If sufficient blood is present in the urine, the blood may form a clot. The clot can completely block the flow of urine, causing sudden extreme pain and inability to urinate. Bleeding severe enough to cause such a clot is usually caused by an injury to the urinary tract.

SYM_KN_TESTISBlOODFLOW.name: Lack of Blood Flow to the Testicle
SYM_KN_TESTISBlOODFLOW.description: Lack of blood flow to the testicle

SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_1.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_1.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_2.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_2.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_3.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_3.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_4.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_4.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_1.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_1.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_2.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_2.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_3.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_3.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_4.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_4.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_BPH_DETC_1.name: Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia
SYM_KN_BPH_DETC_1.description: Benign prostatic hyperplasia was detected through a biopsy

SYM_KN_BPH_DETC_2.name: Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia
SYM_KN_BPH_DETC_2.description: Benign prostatic hyperplasia was detected through a biopsy. However the enlarged prostate didn't react to selective α1-blockers so the minimal invasive surgery is necessary.

SYM_KN_BACSCRO.name: Bacteria Have Been Found in the Scrotum
SYM_KN_BACSCRO.description: Bacteria have been found in the scrotum

SYM_KN_CCRCC_1.name: Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma Was Detected
SYM_KN_CCRCC_1.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_CCRCC_21.name: Ccrcc 2
SYM_KN_CCRCC_21.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_CCRCC_22.name: Ccrcc 2
SYM_KN_CCRCC_22.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_CCRCC_31.name: Ccrcc 3
SYM_KN_CCRCC_31.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_CCRCC_32.name: Ccrcc 3
SYM_KN_CCRCC_32.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_CCRCC_4.name: Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma Was Detected
SYM_KN_CCRCC_4.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_VESCI_FIST.name: Vesicovaginal Fistula
SYM_KN_VESCI_FIST.description: Vesicovaginal fistula, is an abnormal fistulous tract extending between the bladder (vesica) and the vagina that allows the continuous involuntary discharge of urine into the vaginal vault.

SYM_KN_URETHRAL_STONES_DETECTED.name: Ureter Stone Detected
SYM_KN_URETHRAL_STONES_DETECTED.description: Ultrasound examination has shown a large stone obstructing one of the ureters (the tubes connecting kidneys and the bladder).

SYM_KN_BLADDER_STONES_DETECTED.name: Bladder Stones Detected
SYM_KN_BLADDER_STONES_DETECTED.description: Ultrasonography has shown multiple stones in the patient's bladder.

SYM_KN_BLADDER_INFECTION.name: Bladder Infection
SYM_KN_BLADDER_INFECTION.description: A bacterial infection of the bladder has been identified.

SYM_KN_BLADDER_LINING_DEFECTED.name: Bladder Lining Defect
SYM_KN_BLADDER_LINING_DEFECTED.description: Cystography has found various defects in the lining of the bladder.

SYM_KN_KIDNEY_TISSUE_INFLAMMED.name: Kidney Tissue Inflamed
SYM_KN_KIDNEY_TISSUE_INFLAMMED.description: Biopsy has detected an inflammation of a kidney.

SYM_KN_TUBULUS_INFLAMMED.name: Tubulus Inflammation
SYM_KN_TUBULUS_INFLAMMED.description: Laboratory analysis of biological material taken from a kidney discovered inflammation of the tubular system.

SYM_KN_KIDNEY_INTERSTITIUM_ABSCESS.name: Kidney Interstitium Abscess
SYM_KN_KIDNEY_INTERSTITIUM_ABSCESS.description: MRI examination found an abscess in kidney's interstitium.

SYM_KN_KIDNEY_STONES_DISCOVERED.name: Kidney Stones Discovered
SYM_KN_KIDNEY_STONES_DISCOVERED.description: CT examination discovered large stones inside a kidney.

SYM_KN_E_COLI_TOXIN_DETECTED.name: E. Coli Toxin Detected
SYM_KN_E_COLI_TOXIN_DETECTED.description: Stool analysis has detected toxin produced by E. coli.

SYM_KN_BLUNT_KIDNEY_CONTUSION.name: Blunt Kidney Contusion
SYM_KN_BLUNT_KIDNEY_CONTUSION.description: A CT scan has revealed a contusion of the patient's kidney tissue.

SYM_KN_KIDNEY_PROGRESSIVE_DAMAGE.name: Kidney Progressive Damage
SYM_KN_KIDNEY_PROGRESSIVE_DAMAGE.description: CT examination has discovered progressive damage to the kidney tissue.

SYM_KN_KIDNEY_TISSUE_DAMAGED.name: Kidney Tissue Damaged
SYM_KN_KIDNEY_TISSUE_DAMAGED.description: MRI of kidney tissue has proven a certain level of damage.

EXM_CRP_CA.name: CA Blood Sampling
EXM_CRP_CA.description: A Cancer Antigen (CA) test is a blood test used to help diagnose and manage certain types of cancers. Tumour markers are substances, usually proteins, that are produced by the body in response to cancer growth or by the cancer tissue itself. Some tumour markers are specific for one type of cancer, while others are seen in several cancer types. Many of the well-known markers are seen in non-cancerous conditions as well as cancer. Consequently, they cannot be used to diagnose cancer. \n\nRequired Lab: Haematology

EXM_CRP_MINERALT_SAMPLE.name: Comprehensive Mineral Panel Sampling
EXM_CRP_MINERALT_SAMPLE.description: The Comprehensive Mineral Panel includes testing the blood for five minerals and is used to detect deficiencies or overexposure (toxicity) across the mineral spectrum. This panel includes testing levels of the following five minerals: Calcium, Copper, Magnesium, Manganese and Zinc. \n\nRequired Lab: Haematology

EXM_CRP_PELVIC_EXAM.name: Pelvic Exam
EXM_CRP_PELVIC_EXAM.description: A pelvic exam is a doctor's visual and physical examination of a woman's reproductive organs. During the exam, the doctor inspects the vagina, cervix, fallopian tubes, vulva, ovaries, and uterus.

EXM_CRP_DRE_EXAM.name: Digital Rectal Exam (DRE)
EXM_CRP_DRE_EXAM.description: A digital rectal exam (DRE) is a test for both men and women. It allows a doctor to check the lower rectum, pelvis, and lower belly for cancer and other health problems like prostate cancer.

EXM_CRP_SEIN.name: Breast Examination
EXM_CRP_SEIN.description: Visual inspection and palpation of breast.

EXM_KN_INORCHIECTOMY.name: Inguinal Orchiectomy
EXM_KN_INORCHIECTOMY.description: Inguinal orchiectomy is a specific method of orchiectomy whereby one or both testicles and the full spermatic cord are surgically removed through an incision in the lower lateral abdomen. The procedure is generally performed by a urologist, typically if testicular cancer is suspected.

EXM_KN_INORCHIECTOMY_TEST.name: Testing the Obtained Testicle in Inguinal Orchiectomy
EXM_KN_INORCHIECTOMY_TEST.description: Testing the obtained testicle in inguinal orchiectomy.

EXM_KN_TESTIS_PALP.name: Testis Palpation
EXM_KN_TESTIS_PALP.description: Palpation of the scrotum and its contents will reveal the presence, size, position, and shape of the testicles and their adnexa. The normal testicle is ovoid, smooth, firm, and mildly tender to palpation. The testicle is easily separated from the epididymis, which lies posterior and slightly lateral to the testicle.

TRT_CRP_TARGETED_THERAPY.name: Targeted Therapy
TRT_CRP_TARGETED_THERAPY.description: Targeted therapies are drugs or other substances that block the growth and spread of cancer by interfering with specific molecules. Targeted therapies differ from standard chemotherapy because they are designed to act on specific molecular targets and attack cancer cells without harming healthy cells. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_CHEMOTHERAPY.name: Chemotherapy
TRT_CRP_CHEMOTHERAPY.description: Chemotherapy is a type of cancer treatment that uses one or more anti-cancer drugs as part of a standardized chemotherapy regimen. Chemotherapy may be given with a curative intent, or it may aim to prolong life or to reduce symptoms. \n\nRequired room: Regular Hospitalisation at Oncology dpt.

TRT_CRP_ACTIVE_MONITOR.name: Active Monitoring
TRT_CRP_ACTIVE_MONITOR.description: Active monitoring is when the oncologist recommends no treatment but monitors the patient's health with regular check-ups and blood tests. This is sometimes called “careful monitoring” or “watch and wait”. \n\nRequired room: Any Oncology office

TRT_KN_SHOCK_WAVE_LITHOTRIPSY.name: Shock Wave Lithotripsy
TRT_KN_SHOCK_WAVE_LITHOTRIPSY.description:
A procedure commonly carried out to break stones lodged in the urethra (the tube connecting a kidney to the bladder). It is non-surgical treatment option using high energy ultrasound shock waves to broke stones into dust, allowing them to pass. 

Required room: Sonography unit at Urology dpt 

Required equipment: Therapy shockwave

TRT_KN_ALPHA_BLOCKERS.name: Selective Α1-Blockers
TRT_KN_ALPHA_BLOCKERS.description: Selective α1-blockers. They include alfuzosin, doxazosin, silodosin, tamsulosin, terazosin, and naftopidil

TRT_KN_5A_REDUCTASE_INHIBITORS.name: 5α-Reductase Inhibitors
TRT_KN_5A_REDUCTASE_INHIBITORS.description: The 5α-reductase inhibitors finasteride and dutasteride may also be used in men with BPH. These medications inhibit the 5α-reductase enzyme, which, in turn, inhibits production of DHT, a hormone responsible for enlarging the prostate.

TRT_KN_PDE5_INHIBITORS.name: PDE5 Inhibitors
TRT_KN_PDE5_INHIBITORS.description: PDE5 inhibitors sildenafil (Viagra), vardenafil (Levitra) and tadalafil (Cialis) are prescription drugs which are taken by mouth.

TRT_KN_IMMUNO_SUPRES.name: Immunosuppressiva
TRT_KN_IMMUNO_SUPRES.description: Immunosuppressiva are drugs that inhibit activity of the immune system, to make sure that the body doesn't reject the new organ.

TRT_KN_DIALYSIS.name: Dialysis
TRT_KN_DIALYSIS.description:
Is a process of removing the waste products from the cells metabolism from the bloodstream, when the kidneys are not able to maintain this function. 

Required room: Dialysis ward at Urology dpt 

Required equipment: Dialysis machine

TRT_KN_DIALYSIS_2.name: Dialysis
TRT_KN_DIALYSIS_2.description:
Is a process of removing the waste products from the cells metabolism from the bloodstream, when the kidneys are not able to maintain this function. 

Required room: Dialysis ward at Urology dpt 

Required equipment: Dialysis machine

TRT_KN_URN_CATH.name: Urinary Catheter
TRT_KN_URN_CATH.description: Urinary catheter: draining urine from the urinary bladder as in urinary catheterization.

TRT_KN_TRANS_REPAIR.name: Transvaginally Repair
TRT_KN_TRANS_REPAIR.description: Transvaginally repair: A procedure done through the vagina so no big incision is needed.

TRT_KN_ORCHIOPEXY.name: Orchiopexy
TRT_KN_ORCHIOPEXY.description: Orchiopexy is a surgery to move an undescended testicle into the scrotum and permanently fix it there. Orchiopexy typically also describes the surgery used to resolve testicular torsion.

TRT_KN_ORCHIECTOMY.name: Orchiectomy
TRT_KN_ORCHIECTOMY.description: Orchiectomy is a surgical procedure in which one or both testicles are removed (bilateral orchiectomy). The surgery is typically performed as treatment for testicular cancer, in some cases of testicular torsion, as a gender-affirming procedure for some trans women, and is sometimes used in the management of advanced prostate cancer.

TRT_KN_TURP.name: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate
TRT_KN_TURP.description: Transurethral resection of the prostate is thought to be the most effective approach for improving urinary symptoms and urinary flow, however, this surgical procedure may be associated with complications in up to 20% of men.

TRT_KIDN_TRANS.name: Kidney Transplantation
TRT_KIDN_TRANS.description: Kidney transplantation or renal transplantation is the organ transplant of a kidney into a patient with end-stage kidney disease. Kidney transplantation is typically classified as deceased-donor or living-donor transplantation depending on the source of the donor organ.

TRT_KN_VASECTOMY.name: Vasectomy
TRT_KN_VASECTOMY.description: Vasectomy is a surgical procedure for male sterilization or permanent contraception. During the procedure, the male vasa deferentia are cut and tied or sealed so as to prevent sperm from entering into the urethra and thereby prevent fertilization of a female through sexual intercourse.

TRT_KN_PARTNEPH.name: Partial Nephrectomy
TRT_KN_PARTNEPH.description: Nephrectomy is the surgical removal of a kidney. The procedure is done to treat kidney cancer as well as other kidney diseases and injuries. Nephrectomy is also done to remove a healthy kidney from a donor for transplantation. In partial nephrectomy, only the diseased or injured portion of the kidney is removed.

TRT_KN_RADNEPH.name: Radical Nephrectomy
TRT_KN_RADNEPH.description: Nephrectomy is the surgical removal of a kidney. The procedure is done to treat kidney cancer as well as other kidney diseases and injuries. Nephrectomy is also done to remove a healthy kidney from a donor for transplantation. Radical nephrectomy involves removing the entire kidney, along with a section of the tube leading to the bladder, the gland that sits atop the kidney , and the fatty tissue surrounding the kidney.

TRT_KN_RADNEPH_INFVECA.name: Radical Nephrectomy With Inferior Vena Cava Thrombectomy
TRT_KN_RADNEPH_INFVECA.description: Nephrectomy is the surgical removal of a kidney. The procedure is done to treat kidney cancer as well as other kidney diseases and injuries. Radical nephrectomy with inferior vena cava (IVC) thrombectomy is used, when the cancer has grown into nearby veins (as with some stage III cancers). Your surgeon may need to cut open these veins to remove all of the cancer. This may require putting you on bypass (a heart-lung machine), so that the heart can be stopped for a short time to remove the cancer from the large vein leading to the heart.

TRT_KN_BLAD.name: Bladder Surgery
TRT_KN_BLAD.description: Bladder surgery is done to resolve trauma to the bladder.

DIA_COU.name: Calculus of Ureter
DIA_COU.description: Kidney stones, also known as urolithiasis, are usually concretions of calcium. Even though the stones usually pass on their own through the urinary tract, they can sometimes get stuck in the ureter, which is an extremely painful condition.

DIA_CIB.name: Calculus in Bladder
DIA_CIB.description: Bladder stones can be formed in the bladder due to a mineralization of concentrated urine. There will only be symptoms if the flow of urine is compromised.

DIA_ACS.name: Urinary Tract Infection
DIA_ACS.description: UTF, or cystitis, is an infection of the bladder usually by E. coli; a type of bacteria commonly found in the gastrointestinal (GI) tract. It is a condition a hundred times more common in women due to a shorter urinary tract.

DIA_INC.name: Interstitial Cystitis
DIA_INC.description: Interstitial cystitis is also called painful bladder syndrome.

DIA_ANS.name: Glomerulonephritis
DIA_ANS.description: Glomerulonephritis refers to inflammation of the filtering unit of the kidneys. It can be caused by an infection or autoimmunity.

DIA_TIN.name: Interstitial Nephritis
DIA_TIN.description: Interstitial nephritis is an inflammation of the interstitial tissue of the kidneys. It can be caused by an infection or as an adverse reaction to medication.

DIA_PNS.name: Pyelonephritis
DIA_PNS.description: Pyelonephritis is inflammation of the kidney, usually due to ascending bacteria spreading up through the urinary tract.

DIA_ARF.name: Acute Renal Failure
DIA_ARF.description: Acute renal failure refers to an abrupt loss of kidney function, effectively seizing the ability to filter the blood and produce urine. The causes can be many, but are usually due to extensive tissue damage or decreased kidney blood flow.

DIA_CKD.name: Chronic Kidney Disease
DIA_CKD.description: Chronic kidney disease refers to a gradual loss of kidney function. The most common cause is untreated diabetes mellitus.

DIA_COK.name: Kidney Stones
DIA_COK.description: Kidney stones, also known as urolithiasis, are usually concretions of calcium. Even though the stones usually pass on their own through the urinary tract, it is an extremely painful condition.

DIA_047.name: HUS
DIA_047.description: Hemolytic uremic syndrome refers to the condition when small blood vessels in kidneys become damaged and inflamed. In most cases an infection of Escherichia coli is responsible.

TRM_KCB.name: Renal Contusion
TRM_KCB.description: Renal contusion refers to bruising of the kidney (latin: ren) after a direct impact to the upper lower back.

TRM_KLP.name: Renal Laceration
TRM_KLP.description: Renal laceration occurs when an object pierces the tissue of the body and tears the kidney.
